pr.title: Add back the CI badges to `README`
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Restored and expanded GitHub Actions status badges in `.github/README.md`. The existing `build-melee` badge was moved below the progress/Discord badges, and new badges were added for `publish-pages` and `publish-packages`, making README status visibility cover build, pages publishing, and package publishing workflows.
what_changed: Updated the README badge block at the top of `.github/README.md`.
Kept the existing Code Progress, Data Progress, and GC/Wii Decompilation Discord badges.
Re-added the `build-melee` GitHub Actions badge using `actions/workflows/build-melee.yml/badge.svg`.
Added a `publish-pages` GitHub Actions badge linked to `actions/workflows/publish-pages.yml`.
Added a `publish-packages` GitHub Actions badge linked to `actions/workflows/publish-packages.yml`.
smart_moves: Grouped operational GitHub Actions badges alongside the project progress and community badges, improving at-a-glance repository health/status visibility.
Used direct GitHub Actions workflow badge URLs and links, matching the standard badge pattern for workflow status.
Included badges for publishing workflows as well as the main build workflow, so failures outside compilation are visible from the README.
decomp_lessons: README status badges are useful project infrastructure for decompilation repositories because they expose whether build and publishing automation is healthy without digging into the Actions tab.
Small documentation-only changes can still support contributor workflow by making CI state and generated artifact/publishing state immediately discoverable.
When adding or restoring badges, prefer stable workflow-file-linked URLs so badge names track specific GitHub Actions workflows.
naming_conventions: Workflow badge labels mirror workflow file stems: `build-melee`, `publish-pages`, and `publish-packages`.
Progress badges use explicit labels `Code` and `Data` via shields.io endpoint parameters.
The README title remains `# Super Smash Bros Melee`.
follow_up_queries: Find README or documentation PRs that update GitHub Actions badges
Find PRs involving publish-pages or publish-packages workflows
Find infrastructure changes that expose CI status in project docs
